title: I Learned to Eat Alone
paragraphs: Years ago, I felt afraid to eat alone in a restaurant.
I thought, 'Everyone is looking at me.'
But really, no one was looking. They were eating.
Slowly, I tried it. One small lunch. Then another.
I started to taste the food more.
It was quiet. It was good.
Now I love eating alone.
It was not lonely. It was peaceful.
translation: 몇 년 전엔, 식당에서 혼자 먹는 게 무서웠어요.
'다들 나를 보고 있어' 라고 생각했어요.
그런데 사실은, 아무도 안 봤어요. 다 먹고 있었어요.
천천히 해봤어요. 작은 점심 한 번. 또 한 번.
음식 맛이 더 느껴졌어요.
조용했어요. 좋았어요.
지금은 혼자 먹는 게 좋아요.
외로운 게 아니었어요. 평화로운 거였어요.

quiz.question: What did the writer think years ago in a restaurant alone?
quiz.questionKo: 몇 년 전 혼자 식당에서 무슨 생각을 했나요?

title: I Learned to Eat Alone
paragraphs: Years ago, I felt afraid and shy to eat alone in a restaurant.
I always thought, 'Everyone in this place is looking at me.'
But the truth was, no one was looking. They were busy eating their own food.
Slowly, I tried it. One small lunch first. Then another, then many.
I started to taste the food more carefully than before.
It was quiet. It was unexpectedly good.
Now I genuinely love eating alone, sometimes more than with friends.
It was never lonely. It was simply peaceful, and I had not known.
translation: 몇 년 전엔, 식당에서 혼자 먹는 게 무섭고 부끄러웠어요.
늘 생각했어요, '여기 모든 사람이 날 보고 있어' 라고.
그런데 사실은, 아무도 안 봤어요. 다 자기 음식 먹느라 바빴어요.
천천히 해봤어요. 작은 점심 한 번 먼저. 그 다음 또 한 번, 그 다음 여러 번.
전보다 음식 맛을 더 신경 써서 느끼기 시작했어요.
조용했어요. 예상치 못하게 좋았어요.
지금은 혼자 먹는 게 진심으로 좋아요. 가끔 친구와 먹는 것보다 더요.
외로운 적이 없었어요. 그냥 평화로운 거였는데, 몰랐던 거예요.

quiz.question: What were other people in the restaurant actually doing?
quiz.questionKo: 식당의 다른 사람들은 사실 뭘 하고 있었나요?

quiz.question: What did the writer learn?
options: It was simply peaceful — and she had not known

title: I Learned to Eat Alone
paragraphs: Years ago, I felt strangely afraid and shy to eat alone in any kind of public restaurant.
I would walk in, sit down, and immediately think, 'Everyone here is watching me eat by myself.'
But the honest truth, I slowly understood, was that no one was looking. They were busy eating their own food, lost in their own conversations or phones.
Slowly, almost by accident, I started trying it. One small bowl of noodles first. Then another, and another, until it stopped feeling like a test.
I began to taste the food more carefully than I ever had with company.
It was quiet, even in busy places. It was unexpectedly, almost embarrassingly good.
Now I genuinely love eating alone, sometimes more than I love eating with friends.
It was never lonely, as it turned out. It was simply peaceful, and I had spent years not knowing the difference.
translation: 몇 년 전엔, 공공 식당이라면 어디든 혼자 먹는 게 이상하게 무섭고 부끄러웠어요.
걸어 들어가, 앉아서, 즉시 생각했어요, '여기 모든 사람이 내가 혼자 먹는 걸 보고 있어' 라고.
그런데 정직한 진실은, 천천히 이해했지만, 아무도 보고 있지 않았어요. 자기 음식 먹느라, 자기 대화나 폰에 빠져 바빴어요.
천천히, 거의 우연히, 해보기 시작했어요. 작은 국수 한 그릇 먼저. 그 다음 또, 또 한 번, 시험처럼 느껴지지 않을 때까지.
일행과 있을 때보다 음식 맛을 더 신경 써서 느끼기 시작했어요.
조용했어요, 분주한 곳에서도. 예상치 못하게, 거의 부끄러울 만큼 좋았어요.
지금은 혼자 먹는 게 진심으로 좋아요. 가끔은 친구와 먹는 것보다 더요.
알고 보니, 외로운 적이 없었어요. 그냥 평화로운 거였는데, 그 차이를 모르고 몇 년을 보냈어요.

quiz.question: What did the writer slowly understand was the truth?
options: No one was looking — they were busy with their own food, conversations, or phones

quiz.question: What had the writer spent years not knowing?
quiz.questionKo: 글쓴이가 몇 년 동안 모르고 지낸 것은?
options: The difference between lonely and peaceful

title: I Learned to Eat Alone
paragraphs: Years ago, I felt strangely afraid and quietly ashamed to eat alone in any kind of public restaurant.
I would walk in, sit down, and immediately announce to myself, 'Everyone here is watching me eat by myself.'
But the honest truth, I slowly came to understand, was that no one was looking. They were busy with their own food, lost in their own conversations and their own quietly glowing phones.
Slowly, almost by accident, I started trying it. One small bowl of noodles first. Then another, and another, until the act stopped feeling like a small public test of my worth.
I began to taste the food more carefully than I ever had with company — registering textures and small spices I would otherwise have missed.
It was quiet, even in busy places. It was unexpectedly, almost embarrassingly, fundamentally good.
Now I genuinely love eating alone, sometimes more than I love eating with friends or coworkers.
It was never lonely, as it slowly turned out. It was simply peaceful, and I had spent years not knowing the difference between the two.
translation: 몇 년 전엔, 공공 식당이라면 어디든 혼자 먹는 게 이상하게 무섭고 조용히 부끄러웠어요.
걸어 들어가, 앉아서, 즉시 자신에게 선언했어요, '여기 모든 사람이 내가 혼자 먹는 걸 보고 있어' 라고.
그런데 정직한 진실은, 천천히 이해하게 됐지만, 아무도 보고 있지 않았어요. 자기 음식, 자기 대화, 조용히 빛나는 자기 폰에 빠져 바빴어요.
천천히, 거의 우연히, 해보기 시작했어요. 작은 국수 한 그릇 먼저. 그 다음 또, 또 한 번, 그 행위가 제 가치를 시험하는 작은 공개 시험처럼 느껴지지 않을 때까지요.
일행과 있을 때보다 음식 맛을 더 신경 써서 느끼기 시작했어요 — 그러지 않으면 놓쳤을 식감과 작은 향신료들을 등록하면서요.
조용했어요, 분주한 곳에서도. 예상치 못하게, 거의 부끄러울 만큼, 근본적으로 좋았어요.
지금은 혼자 먹는 게 진심으로 좋아요. 가끔은 친구나 동료와 먹는 것보다 더요.
천천히 알게 됐는데, 외로운 적이 없었어요. 그냥 평화로운 거였는데, 그 둘의 차이를 모르고 몇 년을 보냈어요.

quiz.question: What did the act of eating alone feel like at first?
quiz.questionKo: 혼자 먹는 행위는 처음 어떻게 느껴졌나요?
options: A small public test of her worth

quiz.question: What did the writer begin to notice about food when alone?
quiz.questionKo: 혼자일 때 음식에 대해 무엇을 알아채기 시작했나요?
options: Textures and small spices she would otherwise have missed

title: I Learned to Eat Alone
paragraphs: Years ago, I felt strangely afraid and quietly ashamed to eat alone in any kind of public, fundamentally social restaurant.
I would walk in, sit down, and immediately announce to myself, with comprehensive certainty, 'Everyone here is watching me eat by myself.'
But the honest truth, I slowly came to elaborate, was that no one was looking. They were busy with their own food, lost in their own conversations and their own quietly glowing phones.

Slowly, almost by preliminary accident, I started trying it. One small bowl of noodles first. Then another, and another, until the act stopped feeling like a small public test of my fundamental worth.
I began to taste the food more carefully than I ever had with company — registering elaborate textures and small spices I would otherwise have comprehensively missed.
It was quiet, even in busy places. It was unexpectedly, almost embarrassingly, fundamentally good in a way I had not been prepared to receive.
Now I genuinely love eating alone, sometimes more than I love eating with friends, coworkers, or family.
It was never lonely, as it slowly turned out. It was simply peaceful, and I had spent years not knowing the fundamental difference between the two states.
translation: 몇 년 전엔, 공공의, 근본적으로 사회적인 식당이라면 어디든 혼자 먹는 게 이상하게 무섭고 조용히 부끄러웠어요.
걸어 들어가, 앉아서, 즉시 포괄적인 확신으로 자신에게 선언했어요, '여기 모든 사람이 내가 혼자 먹는 걸 보고 있어' 라고.
그런데 정직한 진실은, 천천히 풀어내게 됐지만, 아무도 보고 있지 않았어요. 자기 음식, 자기 대화, 조용히 빛나는 자기 폰에 빠져 바빴어요.
천천히, 거의 예비적 우연으로, 해보기 시작했어요. 작은 국수 한 그릇 먼저. 그 다음 또, 또 한 번, 그 행위가 제 근본적 가치를 시험하는 작은 공개 시험처럼 느껴지지 않을 때까지요.
일행과 있을 때보다 음식 맛을 더 신경 써서 느끼기 시작했어요 — 그러지 않으면 포괄적으로 놓쳤을 정교한 식감과 작은 향신료들을 등록하면서요.
조용했어요, 분주한 곳에서도. 예상치 못하게, 거의 부끄러울 만큼, 받을 준비가 안 되어 있던 방식으로 근본적으로 좋았어요.
지금은 혼자 먹는 게 진심으로 좋아요. 가끔은 친구, 동료, 가족과 먹는 것보다 더요.
천천히 알게 됐는데, 외로운 적이 없었어요. 그냥 평화로운 거였는데, 그 두 상태의 근본적 차이를 모르고 몇 년을 보냈어요.

quiz.question: How does the writer describe the goodness she discovered in eating alone?
quiz.questionKo: 혼자 먹기에서 발견한 좋음을 글쓴이는 어떻게 묘사하나요?
options: Fundamentally good in a way I had not been prepared to receive

quiz.question: What two states had the writer not known the difference between?

title: I Learned to Eat Alone
paragraphs: Years ago, I felt strangely afraid and quietly ashamed to eat alone in any kind of public, fundamentally social restaurant where being seen carried unprecedented weight.

I would walk in, sit down, and immediately announce to myself, with comprehensive and ungrounded certainty, 'Everyone here is watching me eat by myself' — a paradigm I had absorbed from somewhere I could not name.

But the honest truth, I slowly came to elaborate, was that no one was looking. They were busy with their own food, lost in their own conversations and their own quietly glowing phones — each in a small fundamental world of their own.

Slowly, almost by preliminary accident, I started trying it. One small bowl of noodles first. Then another, and another, until the act gradually stopped feeling like a small public test of my fundamental worth or social standing.

I began to taste the food more carefully than I ever had with company — registering elaborate textures, the quiet juxtapose of small spices, and the small honest hungers I would otherwise have comprehensively missed.
It was quiet, even in busy places. It was unexpectedly, almost embarrassingly, fundamentally good in a way I had not been prepared, by years of company, to receive.
Now I genuinely love eating alone, sometimes more than I love eating with friends, coworkers, or family — a quiet preference I once would have considered an unprecedented social failure to admit.

It was never lonely, as it slowly turned out, no matter what the old paradigm had insisted. It was simply peaceful, and I had spent years not knowing the fundamental difference between those two quietly opposing states.
translation: 몇 년 전엔, 보여진다는 게 전례 없는 무게를 지니는 공공의, 근본적으로 사회적인 식당이라면 어디든 혼자 먹는 게 이상하게 무섭고 조용히 부끄러웠어요.
걸어 들어가, 앉아서, 즉시 포괄적이고 근거 없는 확신으로 자신에게 선언했어요, '여기 모든 사람이 내가 혼자 먹는 걸 보고 있어' 라고 — 어디서 흡수했는지 이름 붙일 수도 없는 패러다임이었어요.
그런데 정직한 진실은, 천천히 풀어내게 됐지만, 아무도 보고 있지 않았어요. 자기 음식, 자기 대화, 조용히 빛나는 자기 폰에 빠져 바빴어요 — 각자 자기만의 작고 근본적인 세계 안에서요.
천천히, 거의 예비적 우연으로, 해보기 시작했어요. 작은 국수 한 그릇 먼저. 그 다음 또, 또 한 번, 그 행위가 점차 제 근본적 가치나 사회적 위치를 시험하는 작은 공개 시험처럼 느껴지지 않을 때까지요.
일행과 있을 때보다 음식 맛을 더 신경 써서 느끼기 시작했어요 — 정교한 식감, 작은 향신료들의 조용한 병치, 그러지 않으면 포괄적으로 놓쳤을 작고 솔직한 허기를 등록하면서요.
조용했어요, 분주한 곳에서도. 예상치 못하게, 거의 부끄러울 만큼, 몇 년간의 동행으로 받을 준비가 안 되어 있던 방식으로 근본적으로 좋았어요.
지금은 혼자 먹는 게 진심으로 좋아요. 가끔은 친구, 동료, 가족과 먹는 것보다 더요 — 한때는 인정하기에 전례 없는 사회적 실패라고 여겼을 조용한 선호예요.
천천히 알게 됐는데, 옛 패러다임이 뭐라고 주장했든, 외로운 적이 없었어요. 그냥 평화로운 거였는데, 그 두 조용히 대립하는 상태의 근본적 차이를 모르고 몇 년을 보냈어요.

quiz.question: How does the writer describe the old belief that everyone was watching?
quiz.questionKo: 모두가 보고 있다는 옛 믿음을 어떻게 묘사하나요?
options: A paradigm I had absorbed from somewhere I could not name

quiz.question: What would the writer once have considered her current preference?
quiz.questionKo: 한때 글쓴이는 자신의 현재 선호를 무엇으로 여겼을까요?
options: An unprecedented social failure to admit
